nodes.name: 小种群风险
nodes.summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

nodes.name: Bothalia: African Biodiversity & Conservation
nodes.summary: Bothalia: African Biodiversity & Conservation是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括荒岛秧鸡，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鹤形目
nodes.summary: 鹤形目（Gruiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联126种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、灰冠鹤、Grey-winged trumpeter等126种鸟类。

nodes.name: 荒岛秧鸡
nodes.summary: 荒岛秧鸡（Inaccessible Island rail，学名 Laterallus rogersi），分类上属于鹤形目，并归入秧鸡科，在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，已关联1个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括峡谷峭壁，主要威胁包括小种群风险。

nodes.name: 峡谷峭壁
nodes.summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

nodes.name: 秧鸡科
nodes.summary: 秧鸡科（Rallidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联103种鸟类，如Tsingy forest rail、Madagascar forest rail、Chestnut forest rail等103种鸟类。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联211种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等211种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: L.|title=Vegetation and checklist of Inaccessible Island, central South Atlantic Ocean, with notes on Nightingale Island|journal=Bothalia|date=1992|volume=22|issue=1|pages=93–109|doi=10.4102/abc.v22i1.828 |doi-access=free}} The Inaccessible Island rail can also be found in upland fern-bush heath, dominated by wind-stunted tree-ferns (Blechnum palmiforme) and in the island forest in the central plateau which is dominated by Island Cape myrtle (Phylica arborea)—which can reach where sheltered—and Blechnum palmiforme. In both these habitats the population is estimated to be two birds per hectare. It will also forage among boulders on the beaches, but has not been found in the short dry grasses on the cinder cones (the scientists making the observations cautioned that this does not mean that they never use the habitat). It frequently uses natural cavities among boulders or tunnels through grasses created by frequent use to move around while concealed

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It reaches its highest densities in fields of tussock grass (Spartina arundinacea), with 10 birds per hectare, and in tussock grass mixed with ferns (Blechnum penna-marina) and sedges, with 15 birds per hectare. This habitat is found close to the shore and surrounds most of the island on the steep cliffs.{{cite journal|last1=Roux|first1=J

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The species is nevertheless considered vulnerable by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN), due to its single small population, which would be threatened by the accidental introduction of mammalian predators such as rats or cats
